title: Marketing Designer & Video Editor (Facebook Ads)

evidence: image 1 (fd_logo): Flash Delivery logo present and on-brand for the delivery platform
ad_copy concepts 2,3,5 reference merchants 'Nights Out Express' and 'The Late Stop' — neither exists in brand_pack.json or as supplied logos
deductions: Three of six ad concepts target phantom merchants not in the asset set, blocking fill_text for those cards
No doorstep/delivery photo supplied despite two concepts (id 1,6) explicitly pairing with 'delivery_doorstep.jpg'
Merchant brands are generic, not specific UK chains mentioned in brief.
Ad copy refers to merchants not present in brand_pack.json or merchant_logos.
All assets match Flash Delivery and merchant brands
Ad copy and images align with brief's subject and brand requirements

fix.prompt_delta: Ensure merchant names in ad copy match those in brand_pack.json (Gary's Groceries, The Night Nook).

evidence: ad_copy references 'delivery_doorstep.jpg' (concepts 1,6) and 'product_flatlay.jpg' (concepts 4,5) — neither file is in the asset inventory
brand_pack.json lists only 2 merchants but ad_copy targets 4 distinct merchant names, leaving 2 concepts without any logo or palette asset
deductions: Two named photo assets missing; agent cannot run asset_add_file → fill_text for those concepts without halting
Logos for 'Nights Out Express' and 'The Late Stop' entirely absent; carousel slots for those merchants are unbuildable
Ad copy references merchant names not found in brand_pack.json or merchant_logos.
Ad copy references 'delivery_doorstep.jpg' which is a missing image asset.
Brand guidelines, merchant assets, and ad copy all present
Multiple product/store photos and logos included

fix.problem: Ad copy references missing image and inconsistent merchants.
fix.prompt_delta: Update ad copy to reference existing image assets (e.g., store_product_photos[0], store_product_photos[1], store_product_photos[2]) and correct merchant names.

evidence: image 2 (merchant_logos[0]) and image 3 (merchant_logos[1]) have white/light backgrounds — background_removal step is feasible but logos are raster PNGs on white, not transparent, requiring an extra agent step
store_product_photos (images 4,5) are full-scene renders at 1024x1024 — suitable for image_crop_and_resize to all three aspect ratios without upscaling
deductions: Merchant logos lack transparency; agent must run background-removal before compositing, adding an unplanned step
No raw video clips present; video_create_quick_cut and video_resize steps in the workflow have zero input media
Ad copy merchant names do not map to brand_pack.json or merchant_logos, blocking fill_text.
Ad copy 'pairs_with_photo' references specific filenames not consistently named or present.
All images are 1024x1024, suitable for cropping/resizing
Ad copy structured for template fill and cropping workflow

fix.problem: Ad copy merchant names and photo references are inconsistent.
fix.prompt_delta: Align merchant names in ad_copy.json with brand_pack.json and use generic photo references or actual asset keys.

evidence: images 4,5 are hyper-clean CGI convenience store renders — no real UK corner-shop signage, no Premier/SPAR fascia, overly perfect lighting
image 6 (store_product_photos[2]) is a stock-style grocery flatlay — plausible but generic, not tied to any named merchant's hero products
deductions: CGI store exteriors would read as fake in Facebook ads targeting real UK store owners who know what their shops look like
No photo matches Gary's Groceries hero products (artisan bread, organic milk, local honey) specifically — misses brand_pack data
Merchant logos (image 2, image 3) appear generic and synthetic, not typical for real UK convenience stores.
Store photos (image 4, image 5) are overly polished and appear AI-generated, lacking real-world authenticity.
Store/product photos are realistic and context-appropriate
Logos and brand assets are polished, as expected from a real client

fix.problem: Merchant logos are generic and lack realism for UK convenience stores.
fix.prompt_delta: Regenerate merchant logos to be more realistic and representative of typical UK convenience store branding.

evidence: brand_pack.json declares primary colour #FFD700 (Flash Yellow) but the brief/persona specifies primary #0047AB (Flash Blue); fd_logo (image 1) uses blue as dominant — JSON and persona contradict each other
ad_copy merchant names (Nights Out Express, The Late Stop) are entirely absent from brand_pack.json merchants list, creating a split identity across the asset set
deductions: Palette conflict between brand_pack JSON and persona spec means fill_text colour tokens will be applied inconsistently across 18 variants
Four distinct merchant identities referenced across copy but only two logo assets — half the carousel set has no coherent merchant brand
The brand_pack.json defines a completely different color palette for Flash Delivery than specified in the brief's brand/persona.
Merchant logos (image 2, image 3) are generic and do not visually align with the Flash Delivery brand or typical UK convenience stores.
Consistent palette, fonts, and brand voice across all assets
Merchant assets harmonize with Flash Delivery brand

fix.problem: Flash Delivery brand palette in brand_pack.json contradicts brief.
fix.prompt_delta: Update Flash Delivery palette in brand_pack.json to match: #0047AB (primary), #FF4500 (secondary), #FFD700 (accent), #333333 (secondary).

evidence: Decision to exclude raw video clips is sound and honestly scoped — static pipeline only is a defensible pilot call

Decision to assume 6 concepts x 3 ratios = 18 variants is reasonable but the 6 concepts in ad_copy reference 4 merchants while only 2 merchant asset sets exist — the count assumption is built on a broken foundation
deductions: No assumption recorded for the palette conflict between brand_pack.json and the persona spec — a real executor would be blocked
Phantom merchant names in ad_copy were not flagged as an assumption or gap — they will silently break 3 of 6 concept executions
The brand_pack.json incorrectly defines the Flash Delivery brand palette, contradicting the brief's explicit definition.
The ad_copy.json refers to merchant names not provided as assets or defined in brand_pack.json.
Assumptions about video and static pipeline are justified and documented
Variation count and aspect ratio decisions are sound

fix.problem: Brand palette in brand_pack.json is incorrect.
fix.prompt_delta: Correct Flash Delivery palette in brand_pack.json to match brief's specified hex codes and roles.

top_risks: CLIENT-REJECT (sev 9): The brand colour palette is contradicted between the coherence anchor (#0047AB primary) and brand_pack.json (Flash Yellow #FFD700 as primary) — I cannot approve a handoff where the agent will be fed conflicting brand guidelines and produce ads with the wrong primary colour from day one.

AGENT CHOKE on 'brand_pack': The agent's fill_text and template-colouring steps will ingest brand_pack.json and apply Flash Yellow as the primary colour, directly contradicting the #0047AB Flash Blue declared in the brief's brand anchor — causing every single one of the 18 static variants to render with the wrong dominant colour before a human can catch it.
Completeness 4/10: Two named photo assets missing; agent cannot run asset_add_file → fill_text for those concepts without halting
Realism 5/10: CGI store exteriors would read as fake in Facebook ads targeting real UK store owners who know what their shops look like
qc_gaps: Per-asset self-QC averages 9.5/10, but package Task Fit scored 6 — assets are individually strong yet wrong/insufficient for the task.
Per-asset self-QC averages 9.5/10, but package Realism scored 5 — assets are individually strong yet wrong/insufficient for the task.
Per-asset self-QC averages 9.5/10, but package Executability scored 6 — assets are individually strong yet wrong/insufficient for the task.
